races.name: Giro d'Italia — Stage 5
races.finish_type: Punchy uphill finish — final km at 2.5%, technical run-in
races.description:
Stage 5 of the 109th Giro d'Italia — the first genuinely hard day on home Italian soil, and the first significant opportunity for GC moves in week one. Starting from Praia a Mare on the Calabrian Riviera, the route climbs inland through 203km and 3,724m of elevation across the mountains of Basilicata to finish in Potenza (823m), the regional capital perched high in the Apennines. Key climbs include the Cat-3 Prestieri (13km @ 4.6%), the Red Bull Kilometre at around the 170km mark, and the category-2 Montagna Grande di Viggiano (6.6km @ 9.2%) — the hardest climb of the day, cresting with ~48km to go. The finishing circuit in Potenza features a sharp 1.3km climb at 7% (max 12%) with 4km to go, then a descent and a slight uphill drag to the line — too technical for pure sprinters, but suited to puncheurs and opportunists. The stage ProfileScore of 203 signals a day that should see a long-range break succeed, though Lidl-Trek, riding for Maglia Rosa Ciccone, may animate proceedings.

Giro story so far (Stages 1–4): Stage 1 (Nessebar→Burgas, Bulgaria) was won by Paul Magnier (Soudal Quick-Step), the teenage French sensation taking the first Maglia Rosa. Stage 2 (Burgas→Veliko Tarnovo) was won by Guillermo Thomas Silva who took the race lead. Stage 3 (Plovdiv→Sofia) saw Silva hold on. After the Rest Day on May 11, Stage 4 (Catanzaro→Cosenza) was won by Jhonatan Narváez (UAE-XRG); Giulio Ciccone (Lidl-Trek) cleverly sprinted for time bonuses on the stage to leap into the Maglia Rosa — his career first. Jan Christen (INEOS Grenadiers) leads the Maglia Bianca (young riders). Giulio Pellizzari (Red Bull-BORA) has moved ahead of Jonas Vingegaard in the provisional GC via bonus seconds ahead of the mountains. Notable DNFs: Kaden Groves (DNF Stage 4), Santiago Buitrago (DNF Stage 2), Arnaud De Lie (illness); Richard Carapaz also withdrew.

gc_after_stage_4.maglia_rosa: Giulio Ciccone (Lidl-Trek) — first career pink jersey
gc_after_stage_4.notes: Pellizzari (RBH) ahead of Vingegaard (VLB) on GC via bonus seconds. Ciccone leads, with Narváez, Pellizzari, Vingegaard, Christen among contenders. Silva (former leader) dropped back.

stage_winners_so_far: S1 (Nessebar→Burgas, 147km): Paul Magnier (Soudal Quick-Step) — sprint, takes first Maglia Rosa
S2 (Burgas→Veliko Tarnovo, 221km): Guillermo Thomas Silva — sprint, takes Maglia Rosa
S4 (Catanzaro→Cosenza, 138km): Jhonatan Narváez (UAE-XRG) — Ciccone takes Maglia Rosa via bonuses
stage_favourites: Jan Christen (INEOS Grenadiers) — puncheur-climber, Maglia Bianca leader
Giulio Ciccone (Lidl-Trek) — pink jersey, excellent puncheur
Lennert Van Eetvelt (Lotto Intermarché) — explosive puncheur
Giulio Pellizzari (Red Bull-BORA-hansgrohe) — GC contender, punchy finisher
Jonas Vingegaard (Visma | Lease a Bike) — could control via Ciccone pace

broadcasts.notes: TNT Sports and HBO Max carry all 21 stages live. Pre/post-stage coverage via 'The Breakaway'. Subscription from £25.99/mo (annual).
HBO Max Standard ($18.49/mo) or Premium ($20.99/mo). Also airs on TruTV linear. Warner Bros. Discovery holds exclusive US rights through 2029.
FloBikes subscription from CAN$49.99/mo or CAN$215.88/year. All stages live.
FREE with registration. All 21 stages live on SBS Viceland and SBS On Demand with English commentary. Daily highlights replayed mornings.
Free-to-air. Rai Sport HD: 'Giro Mattina' + 'Prima Diretta'. Rai 2: 'Giro in Diretta' 14:00–16:15, then 'Giro all'Arrivo'. 'Processo alla Tappa' post-stage. Available on RaiPlay.
Free-to-air via SRG. RSI (Italian) and SRF (German).
Eurosport covers the Giro across most European markets. Discovery+ streaming.

races.name: Tour de Hongrie — Stage 1
races.finish_type: Bunch sprint — flat finish, wind may play role
races.description: The 47th Tour de Hongrie opens with a flat 143.1km stage from Gyula (in Békés County — a first for the race since 2005) to Békéscsaba, completing 2.5 laps of a circuit around the area. With just 163m of climbing and a ProfileScore of 1, this is a pure sprint day where wind across the open Hungarian plains is the main tactical factor. The race runs May 13–17 (Gyula → Veszprém, 836km total), concluding in Veszprém, named European Capital of Sport 2026. It's a UCI ProSeries 2.Pro race with a quality startlist including 4 WorldTeams. Grand Start and team presentation held on May 12 at Gyula Castle. Key sprinters: Tim Merlier (Soudal Quick-Step), Fernando Gaviria, Juan Sebastián Molano (UAE-XRG), Albert Withen Philipsen, Max Kanter (XDS Astana). Punchy finishers who can win on harder stages: Benoît Cosnefroy (UAE-XRG). Queen stage is Stage 4 (May 16, Mohács→Pécs, 188km) finishing on the Lapis/Bárány Road climb.
key_riders: Juan Sebastián Molano (UAE Team Emirates-XRG) — sprint

broadcasts.notes: Hungarian public broadcaster M4 Sport carries the race live. Free-to-air.
PCS LiveStats provides live timing and race data globally. No confirmed major international broadcast; check Eurosport in select markets.

races.name: Navarra Women's Elite Classic
races.finish_type: Circuit sprint / small group — puncheur finish after relentless climbs
races.description: The 8th edition of the Navarra Women's Elite Classic — a one-day 1.Pro race in the Basque/Navarran heartland, starting and finishing in Pamplona. The 133.4km route passes through 20 towns with nine categorised climbs (including Biurrun, Artajona, Tirapu and Arlegui with ramps up to 21%), relentlessly breaking up the peloton. The final 10km are approached via the Muro de Arlegui (3.6km @ 2.8%), cresting 9.2km from the finish, before a punchy return to Pamplona. 18 teams: 5 UCI WWT (Movistar, Lidl-Trek, Liv AlUla Jayco, EF Education-Oatly, Picnic PostNL) + 3 Pro + 8 Continental + 1 national. Organised by M2 Eventos. First race for many riders post-Vuelta Femenina. Key favourites: Fleur Moors (Lidl-Trek), Liane Lippert (Movistar), Cat Ferguson (Movistar), Ruby Roseman-Gannon (Liv AlUla Jayco). Paula Ostiz (Movistar) makes her return from injury in her home race.
key_riders: Fleur Moors (Lidl-Trek) — spring sensation, close to first win of 2026
Liane Lippert (Movistar Team) — home race, puncheur
Paula Ostiz (Movistar Team) — injury return, Pamplona native

broadcasts.notes: Eurosport and Discovery+ carry the race internationally. Coverage typically from the decisive final 40-50km. Spanish, English, French, German, Italian, Dutch and Japanese commentary available.
Eurosport España is the primary carrier for this race in Spain.
Full live timing and stats globally via PCS.

races.name: Baku-Khankendi Azerbaijan Cycling Race — Stage 4
races.description: Stage 4 of the 7th edition of the Baku-Khankendi Azerbaijan Cycling Race (formerly Tour d'Azerbaïdjan, revived in 2026 after a 9-year absence). A UCI 2.1 race running May 10–14 from Baku to Khankendi across 5 stages, notably passing through recently liberated territories in stages 4–5. GC leader: Yevgeniy Fedorov, who won Stage 2 (Baku→İsmayıllı) and has held the lead through Stage 3 (Gabala→Mingachevir, won by Gleb Syritsa). Stage 1 (Baku→Sumqayıt) was won by Aliaksei Shnyrko. The race features 1 WorldTeam (Cofidis or similar), 4 ProTeams, and 17 Continental teams plus national squads. Top riders by PCS ranking: Henok Mulubrhan, Yevgeniy Fedorov, Aaron Gate. Final stage (May 14) finishes in Khankendi.
stage_winners_so_far: S2 (Baku→İsmayıllı): Yevgeniy Fedorov — takes GC lead
S3 (Gabala→Mingachevir): Gleb Syritsa — Fedorov holds GC

broadcasts.notes: Local Azerbaijani broadcaster. International coverage limited to PCS LiveStats.
